title: Mail a Package
summary: Send a package through the postal service with proper labeling and postage.
learnerPromise: You will be able to prepare, address, and send a package through the mail with the correct postage and labeling.
whyItMatters: Mailing packages lets you send gifts, return items, ship sold goods, and share physical objects with others. Doing it correctly ensures your package arrives safely and on time.
realLifeUses: You sold an item online and need to ship it to the buyer
You want to send a gift to a friend or family member who lives far away
You need to return a purchase that did not fit or work
You are mailing important documents that need to arrive securely
materialsNeeded: A pen for writing the address
Postage or money to buy postage
Optional: bubble wrap, newspaper, or padding material
result: A sealed, labeled, and postage-paid package ready for drop-off or pickup.
youWillLearn: How to choose the right box or envelope for your item
How to pack an item so it does not break or shift
How to label a package with a clear delivery and return address
How to buy and apply the correct postage
miniChallenge: Pack a small item securely, write the delivery and return address on the package, and buy the correct postage at a post office or online.
steps: Choose a box or envelope that fits your item with a little room for padding
Wrap or pad the item so it cannot move around or break
Seal the package completely with tape on all open edges
Write the recipient's address clearly on the front and your return address on the top-left
Buy postage at a post office, online, or a shipping station and attach it to the package
completionCriteria: The package is sealed and the address is complete and readable
Correct postage is attached and the package is dropped off or ready for pickup
commonProblems: The package is too heavy or large for the postage you bought — check weight and size limits before buying
Fragile items break in transit — use more padding than you think you need
The address is smudged or unclear — use a permanent marker and print legibly
tips: Weigh your package on a home scale or at the post office to get the right postage
If the item is valuable, ask about tracking numbers and insurance options
Take a photo of the sealed, labeled package before you drop it off for your records
